overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a focused and active academic and professional social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky. His content primarily revolves around computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. With a combined total of 3,580 followers and an average engagement rate of approximately 5.32% per post, he effectively reaches a targeted audience. LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count, while Bluesky demonstrates the highest per-post engagement.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new papers, research grants, algorithm releases (e.g., CooRnet), and discussions on methodological approaches.
Focus on topics such as coordinated link sharing, information operations, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and general disinformation studies.
Posts related to hiring (postdoctoral researchers), PhD opportunities, and conference presentations.

recommendations: Continue to cross-post major announcements (new papers, grants, hiring) across all platforms, tailoring the content slightly for each platform's audience (e.g., more professional on LinkedIn, more community-oriented on Mastodon/Bluesky).
Leverage Bluesky's strong engagement for high-impact research announcements and fostering direct community discussion, as it currently yields the highest per-post engagement.

On LinkedIn, capitalize on the larger follower base and the high performance of grant/hiring news by potentially sharing more frequent updates on project progress or team achievements to maintain engagement beyond major announcements.
Encourage more direct interaction by posing questions in posts, particularly on platforms like Bluesky where the 'reply for details' prompt on opportunity posts has shown good results.
Explore creating short, engaging summaries or visual abstracts for research releases to improve shareability and comprehension across all platforms.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most engaging platform with an average post engagement rate of 6.14%. Content related to new publications and research opportunities performs exceptionally well, indicating a highly active and receptive audience for academic news and career-focused content.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves as a dedicated academic channel, with specific tool releases (CooRnet) and methodological discussions receiving moderate engagement (average 4.55%). Its smaller, focused follower base suggests it's a valuable platform for niche academic discourse.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower count (1850) and strong performance on professional news, particularly grant announcements and hiring calls (average 4.87%). This platform is excellent for reaching a broader professional network and attracting talent.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
